name: Professional Fundraiser Contract Compliance Check
summary: Audits agreements with professional solicitors, fundraising counsel, and commercial co-venturers to extract disclosure data required for state charity annual reports and verify that contracts include state-mandated clauses. The workflow classifies the fundraising relationship, verifies vendor registration status, extracts compensation and custody-of-funds terms, checks for required contract provisions across jurisdictions, and produces disclosure-ready narrative snippets, a gap/remediation log, and an audit-ready evidence package.
content:
# Professional Fundraiser Contract Compliance Check

**Slug:** professional-fundraiser-contract-compliance-check
**Version:** 1.0

> **Use case:** You are preparing state charity annual reports or renewals (and related campaign filings) and must disclose third-party fundraising relationships and confirm the underlying contracts include state-required terms. This skill also serves as a due-diligence tool when onboarding new professional fundraisers, solicitors, or commercial co-venturers.

---

## Assumptions Used

1. **Jurisdictional variability is material.** Requirements for professional solicitors, fundraising counsel, and commercial co-venturers vary by state. This skill provides a structured method and common clause set, and it **flags** where state-specific statutory text must be verified.
2. **Entity type.** The organization is a 501(c)(3) or 501(c)(4) charitable organization subject to state charitable solicitation laws.
3. **Contract set may be incomplete.** If only a master agreement is available, SOWs, inserts, and amendments may contain key financial terms. Missing documents are treated as compliance risks.
4. **Annual report forms differ.** This skill targets the most common disclosure fields (e.g., California RRF-1/CT-2CF, New York CHAR500/CHAR007, Form 990 Schedule G). Exact form field names and required attachments must be confirmed per state.
5. **Legislative change.** Charitable solicitation statutes are subject to frequent amendment. Verify current requirements before relying on any checklist item.
6. **Not legal advice.** This workflow supports legal and compliance review. Consult qualified counsel specializing in charitable solicitation law for state-specific interpretation and enforcement risk.

---

## Open Items / Needed Inputs

Provide what you have; the workflow proceeds with partial inputs.

- **States in scope:** Where the charity is registered and/or solicits (or intends to solicit).
- **Filing target(s):** Annual report/renewal, campaign notice, co-venturer filing, professional solicitor filing, financial report of campaign, etc.
- **Relationship type candidates:** Professional solicitor, professional fundraiser/counsel, commercial co-venturer, marketing vendor, call center, platform, influencer/affiliate.
- **Contract documents:** Executed agreement(s), amendments, SOWs, exhibits, fee schedules, call scripts, creative approvals, data processing addenda.
- **Operational artifacts:** Donation flow diagram, merchant processor agreements, bank deposit controls, reconciliation process, campaign financial reports.
- **Registration IDs:** Fundraiser/solicitor/co-venturer registration numbers (if known) and charity registration IDs per state.
- **Solicitation start date:** Needed to check "Notice of Intent" / contract filing deadlines.
- **Point-of-solicitation disclosure text:** Sample of the disclosure used by the fundraiser during calls or asks.

---

# Phase 0: Minimum-Input Alignment (MANDATORY)

## Checkpoint A — Pre-Draft Intake Snapshot

**Answer if you can; if not, defaults apply and the workflow proceeds.**

| # | Parameter | Default | Options |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Jurisdiction scope** | Multi-state (build state-agnostic baseline; flag state-specific checks) | Single state / 3–10 states / Nationwide |
| 2 | **Filing target(s)** | State annual report/renewal disclosures | Annual report/renewal; Initial registration; Campaign notice; Co-venturer filing; Campaign financial report |
| 3 | **Relationship type(s)** | Unknown — classify from facts/contract | Professional solicitor; Fundraising counsel/consultant; Commercial co-venturer; Mixed |
| 4 | **Contract universe** | Master agreement + all SOWs/amendments | Single contract; Multiple vendors; Multiple campaigns |
| 5 | **Output mode** | Full Package | Full Package; Quick Reference; Checklist Only |

**Assumptions if no response:** Multi-state, annual-report focused, mixed relationship types, contract set may be incomplete, Full Package output.

---

# Phase 1: Classify the Relationship & Verify Registration Status

## Objective

Determine whether the third party is acting as a **professional solicitor**, **professional fundraiser/fundraising counsel**, **commercial co-venturer**, or another vendor category — because that classification drives (a) what must be disclosed in annual reports, (b) what contract clauses and filings are legally required, and (c) which state registration obligations apply.

---

## Deliverable 1.1 — Relationship Classification Decision Tree

Use contract language **and** actual operations.

### A. Professional Solicitor (common markers)
- The third party **solicits** donations (calls, mail, door-to-door, online ads with direct ask) **on behalf of** a charitable organization.
- Often **paid** (fee, percent, retainer, per-lead, per-call) and may handle donor data and scripts.
- May or may not take **custody of funds**.
- Typically triggers the **highest** regulatory burden (registration, contract filing, campaign financial reporting, point-of-solicitation disclosures).

### B. Fundraising Counsel / Professional Fundraiser (common markers)
- Provides **advice, planning, consulting, or campaign management** but generally does **not** directly solicit contributions and does **not** have custody of funds.
- If counsel does solicit or takes custody, many states reclassify them as a solicitor, imposing additional requirements.

### C. Commercial Co-Venturer (common markers)
- A **for-profit entity** advertises that a purchase or use of its goods/services will benefit a charity ("$1 per sale goes to X" or "% of proceeds donated").
- The for-profit typically does **not** act as an agent soliciting donations; it markets its own goods/services tied to a charitable benefit.
- State requirements focus on **advertising disclosures** (benefit amount/percentage, campaign duration, charity identity) and **accounting/reporting** to the charity.

### D. Platform / Processor / Vendor (needs careful analysis)
- Donation platform, payment processor, employer matching platform, influencer/affiliate network, "round-up" program.
- May become a solicitor or co-venturer depending on marketing claims and control over solicitation.

> **Escalation trigger:** If classification is unclear or multiple models exist in one campaign (e.g., co-venture plus paid digital solicitation), treat as **highest-risk category** until clarified by counsel.

---

## Deliverable 1.2 — Fundraiser Identity & Registration Verification Matrix

State regulators require that both the charity and the fundraiser are registered and in good standing *before* a contract is signed and *before* solicitation begins.

| Data Point | Contract Reference (Section) | Extracted Value | Verification Source | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Legal name of fundraiser | | | State registry (e.g., NY Charities Bureau) | ☐ Confirmed / ☐ Mismatch | Must match state registry exactly |
| DBA / trade name (if any) | | | | ☐ Confirmed | |
| Principal place of business | | | | ☐ Address match | Needed for Form 990 Sch. G |
| State of formation / entity type | | | Secretary of State DB | ☐ Confirmed | |
| Registration No. (per state) | | | State DB (e.g., NY FR-No, CA CT-No) | ☐ Current / ☐ Expired | Include expiration/renewal dates |
| Registered agent for service | | | | ☐ Confirmed | Necessary for legal notices |
| Bond posted (if required) | | | State bond registry | ☐ Yes / ☐ No / ☐ N/A | Some states require surety bond |
| Charity's own registration in that state | | | State DB | ☐ Current | Must be current before solicitation |

---

## Deliverable 1.3 — Filing/Disclosure Obligation Mapping Worksheet

Populate at a high level; later phases fill the details.

| Item | What to Capture | Why It Matters | Source | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| States involved | Registered / solicited states | Different mandated clauses & filings | Registration map / campaign plan | |
| Relationship type | Solicitor / counsel / co-venturer | Drives required disclosures | Contract + operations | |
| Campaign scope | Dates, channels, audience | Annual report and campaign report triggers | SOW, media plan | |
| Funds flow | Who receives donations and when remitted | Custody-of-funds clauses & reporting | Processor agreement, bank info | |
| Compensation model | %/flat/retainer/per unit | Often disclosed; impacts reasonableness | Fee schedule, invoices | |
| Registration IDs | Vendor registration nos. per state | Frequently required on forms | State registry printouts | |
| Notice of Intent / Contract filing | Pre-solicitation filing deadlines | Often 10–20 days before campaign starts | State statute | |

---

# Phase 2: Contract Data Extraction for Annual-Report Disclosures

## Objective

Extract the specific information typically required for **state annual report/renewal** disclosures about professional fundraisers/solicitors and commercial co-venturers.

---

## Deliverable 2.1 — Disclosure Data Extraction Table

Complete one row per **vendor × campaign × contract/SOW**.

| Field | Extract / Compute | Contract Section / Exhibit | Notes / Flags for Disclosure |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Parties** | | | |
| Legal names & addresses | Charity + vendor legal name; principal business address | Preamble / notices | Confirm entity type and state of formation |
| **Classification** | | | |
| Relationship classification | Solicitor / counsel / co-venturer / other | Definitions / scope of services | If mixed, split into separate rows |
| **Campaign Details** | | | |
| Campaign name/ID | Internal campaign ID | SOW / exhibit | Tie to annual report period |
| Campaign dates | Start/end; renewal terms | Term clause / SOW | Disclose period covering report year |
| Services description | What vendor does (calls, mail, ads, fulfillment) | Scope of work | Include whether "solicitation" occurs |
| **Compensation** | | | |
| Compensation type | % of receipts; flat fee; retainer; per call; CPM; bonuses | Fees exhibit | If multiple structures, break down each |
| Compensation percentage | Exact percentage(s) + base (gross receipts? net?) | Fees exhibit | If not %, describe formula |
| Minimums / guarantees | Minimum fee, guaranteed volume | Fees / SOW | May affect "net proceeds" calculation |
| Expense allocations | Printing, postage, media buys, chargebacks | SOW / reimbursements | Needed for "net to charity" disclosures |
| **Funds & Custody** | | | |
| Custody of funds | Who receives donor funds first | Payment clause | High-risk if vendor receives funds |
| Deposit method | Direct to charity lockbox / joint account / vendor account | Payment clause | Joint accounts may require dual-signature controls |
| Deposit timing | When deposited and where | Payment clause | Check state "within X days" requirement |
| Remittance timing | How quickly funds remitted to charity | Payment clause | Verify operationally with bank records |
| Segregation | Separate account? Trust account? | Payment clause | Flag if contract is silent |
| Deductions | Fees deducted before remittance? | Payment clause | Processor fees, refunds, chargebacks, vendor comp |
| **Other Terms** | | | |
| Use of charity name/logo | Approval rights; brand guidelines | IP/marketing clauses | Important for co-venture advertising compliance |
| Donor list/data | Ownership; permitted uses | Data/privacy exhibit | Some states require record access |
| Records & inspection | Required campaign records; charity access | Recordkeeping clause | Needed for campaign financial reports |
| Scripts/materials approval | Charity approval of scripts/ads | Marketing clause | Often mandated or best practice |
| Required governmental filings | Who files what; cooperation | Compliance clause | Flag missing allocation of responsibilities |
| Vendor registration number(s) | Per-state registration IDs | Vendor-provided + registry | Include expiration/renewal dates |
| Cancellation rights | Charity right to cancel (timing) | Termination clause | Many states mandate specific cancellation window |
| Subcontractors | Use of subs; approvals | Subcontracting clause | Subs may need registration too |
| Insurance / indemnity | Coverage and indemnities | Risk clauses | Not always required by state but risk-relevant |

---

## Deliverable 2.2 — Funds Flow & Custody-of-Funds Map

Create a simple map (text-based is fine) and attach supporting evidence.

**Template (fill in):**

1. **Donor payment methods:** Credit card / ACH / check / cash / text-to-give / online platform.
2. **Donation intake entity:** Vendor / platform / charity / fiscal sponsor.
3. **Processor merchant of record:** Vendor / charity / platform.
4. **Deposit account owner:** Vendor / charity; bank name; account type (operating / trust / segregated).
5. **Timing:** Deposit within ___ days; remittance to charity within ___ days.
6. **Deductions:** Fees deducted before remittance? (Processor fees, refunds, chargebacks, vendor compensation.)
7. **Reconciliation:** Who reconciles, frequency, controls.

**Red flags to note:**
- Vendor receives checks payable to vendor (not charity)
- Commingled accounts
- Remittance timing not stated in contract
- Deductions not transparent
- Charity lacks bank-view or reconciliation rights
- No segregation of charitable funds from vendor's operating funds

---

# Phase 3: State-Mandated Clause & Operational Compliance Audit

## Objective

Check whether the contract includes clauses that are **commonly mandated** (or strongly expected) for professional fundraiser/solicitor/co-venturer arrangements, and whether actual operations match the contractual terms.

> **Important:** This is a **baseline** checklist derived from common state requirements. For each state in scope, confirm the exact required clause language, timing, and filing/notice obligations by consulting the applicable statute.

---

## Deliverable 3.1 — Baseline Mandated-Clause Compliance Checklist

Mark each item for each contract/SOW.

### A. Parties, Scope & Campaign Definition
- [ ] Contract clearly identifies the **charity** and **vendor** (legal names, addresses).
- [ ] Contract defines the **services** and whether the vendor will **solicit contributions**.
- [ ] Contract identifies **campaign(s)** covered (or references SOWs).
- [ ] Contract specifies the **geographic scope** of solicitation.

### B. Compensation & Financial Terms
- [ ] Compensation is stated **clearly** (percentage, fees, and calculation base).
- [ ] If compensation is a percentage, contract defines **"gross receipts" vs. "net proceeds"** (or equivalent).
- [ ] Reimbursable expenses and cost allocations are defined.
- [ ] Any minimums, guarantees, or bonuses are documented.
- [ ] It is possible to compute the **percentage of gross contributions the charity retains** (needed for most state disclosures).

### C. Custody of Funds / Handling of Contributions (High Priority)
- [ ] Contract states **who has custody/control** of contributions at each step.
- [ ] If vendor receives contributions: contract requires **segregation** (separate account) and clear deposit/remittance timing.
- [ ] Contract addresses handling of **cash/checks**, endorsements, and payable-to instructions.
- [ ] Contract addresses **refunds/chargebacks**, processor fees, and reporting.
- [ ] Joint account or lockbox arrangement is documented (if applicable).

### D. Term, Termination & Cancellation Rights
- [ ] Contract term and renewal provisions are clear.
- [ ] Contract includes charity termination rights.
- [ ] Contract includes any **statutorily required cancellation right** (commonly 10–15 days; verify per state — e.g., NY, CA require specific windows).

### E. Recordkeeping, Reporting & Audit/Inspection
- [ ] Vendor must maintain **campaign records** (donations, expenses, scripts, donor communications) for a defined period (commonly 3–7 years).
- [ ] Charity has right to **access/inspect** records and obtain campaign financial reports.
- [ ] Vendor must provide periodic **accountings** (frequency and content defined).
- [ ] Written financial report required within a specified period after campaign end (commonly 90 days; verify by state).

### F. Solicitation Materials, Scripts & Disclosures
- [ ] Charity approval of solicitation scripts/materials is required **before use**.
- [ ] Required **point-of-solicitation disclosures** are addressed (e.g., "This call is being made by [solicitor name] on behalf of [charity]. [X%] of your contribution will be retained by the solicitor.").
- [ ] Commercial co-venture ads include required elements (benefit amount/percentage, campaign duration, charity identity) — verify by state.

### G. Compliance Representations & Registration
- [ ] Vendor represents it is (and will remain) **registered/licensed** where required.
- [ ] Contract requires vendor to provide **registration numbers** and renewal evidence.
- [ ] Contract allocates responsibility for **state filings/notices** (campaign notices, contract filings, Notices of Intent) and cooperation.
- [ ] Contract references filing deadlines (commonly 10–20 days before solicitation begins).

### H. Subcontractors & Downstream Compliance
- [ ] Subcontracting requires charity consent.
- [ ] Subcontractors must meet the same registration/recordkeeping requirements.

### I. Data, Privacy & Donor List Usage
- [ ] Contract addresses donor data ownership and permitted use.
- [ ] Prohibits unauthorized sale/rental of donor lists.
- [ ] Includes security measures and breach notification obligations.

---

## Deliverable 3.2 — State-Specific Clause Matrix (fill-as-you-go)

Use this to track verification for each state in scope.

| State | Relationship Type | Statute / Reg Cite | Required Contract Clauses (summary) | Cancellation Right | Financial Report Deadline | Required Filings / Attachments | Verified (Y/N) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CA | | Bus. & Prof. Code §§ 17510+ | | | | CT-1/CT-2 series | | |
| NY | | Exec. Law Art. 7-A; 13 NYCRR Part 91 | | | | CHAR007 | | |
| FL | | Ch. 496, Fla. Stat. | | | | | | |
| IL | | 225 ILCS 460/ | | | | | | |
| [Add state] | | | | | | | | |

**How to use:** Start with your highest-volume solicitation states. Add statute citations once confirmed. This table is a working document, not a final legal determination.

---

# Phase 4: Remediation Plan, Disclosure-Ready Outputs & Evidence Packaging

## Objective

Turn findings into (1) a disclosure-ready dataset for annual reports, (2) a concrete plan to remediate contract gaps and operational issues, and (3) an evidence package for audit readiness.

---

## Deliverable 4.1 — Gap / Issue Log (actionable)

| Issue ID | Contract / Vendor | Issue Type | Finding | Risk (H/M/L) | Recommended Fix | Owner | Deadline |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | | Missing clause | Contract silent on custody/remittance timing | H | Add amendment specifying deposit/remittance timeline + segregation | Legal | |
| 2 | | Disclosure gap | Compensation % base ambiguous; cannot compute net-to-charity | M | Clarify definition of gross receipts and fee calculation | Vendor Mgmt | |
| 3 | | Registration | Vendor registration IDs missing or expired in [State] | H | Obtain proof; suspend solicitation where required | Compliance | |
| 4 | | Cancellation right | No 15-day cancellation clause (required in NY/CA) | H | Amend contract to add statutory cancellation right | Legal | |
| 5 | | Point-of-solicitation | No disclosure script mandating charity name and % retained | M | Add script approval clause; draft compliant disclosure | Compliance | |
| 6 | | Filing deadline | Notice of Intent not filed before campaign start | H | File retroactively where allowed; assess risk of late filing | Legal | |

---

## Deliverable 4.2 — Disclosure-Ready Narrative Snippets (templates)

Use as starting points; tailor to the specific state form prompts.

### A. Professional Solicitor / Fundraiser Relationship

> "During the reporting period [start date] to [end date], the organization engaged **[Vendor legal name]** (address: **[address]**; registration no.: **[state: ID]**) as a **[professional solicitor / fundraising counsel]** for **[campaign description]**. Compensation was **[X% of gross contributions received / $X flat fee / description of fee structure]** plus **[reimbursable expenses / other]**. Total gross contributions received through this arrangement were $**[amount]**; total fees paid to the solicitor were $**[amount]**; net to the organization was $**[amount]** (**[Y]%** of gross). Contributions were **[received by the organization directly / received by vendor and remitted to organization within [X] days]**."

### B. Commercial Co-Venturer Promotion

> "During the reporting period, **[For-profit legal name]** conducted a commercial co-venture promotion benefiting the organization: **[promotion description]**, running from **[start date]** to **[end date]**. The benefit to the organization was **[$X per sale / X% of sales / other formula]**, subject to **[cap/floor if any]**. Total benefit received: $**[amount]**. Funds were transferred to the organization **[timing/method]**. Where applicable, the co-venturer's registration/filing information: **[state: ID/filing date]**."

---

## Deliverable 4.3 — State Reporting Data Summary Sheet

Maps extracted data to common state form fields.

| Item | Extracted Data | Form 990 Sch. G | CA CT-2CF | NY CHAR007 | Other State Form |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Solicitor / CCV name | | Part I, Line 2a | Part II, Line 1 | Part B | |
| Compensation % or amount | | Part I, Line 2c | Part II, Line 3 | Part B | |
| Gross receipts from campaign | | Part I, Line 2b | | Part B | |
| Net retained by charity | | | | Part B | |
| Custody of funds (Y/N) | | Part I, Line 2b | | | |
| Campaign start date | | | | | |
| Campaign end date | | | | | |
| Vendor registration no. | | | | | |

---

## Deliverable 4.4 — Evidence Binder Checklist (audit-ready)

- [ ] Executed contract + all amendments/SOWs/exhibits.
- [ ] Vendor registration proof and IDs for each relevant state (screenshots/printouts with dates).
- [ ] Charity registration proof for each relevant state.
- [ ] Notice of Intent / contract filing confirmations (where required).
- [ ] Campaign materials: scripts, mailers, landing pages, ads, point-of-solicitation disclosures.
- [ ] Funds flow proof: processor statements, bank deposit records, remittance confirmations.
- [ ] Campaign accounting: gross receipts, expenses, net to charity, refunds/chargebacks.
- [ ] Vendor's written financial report of campaign (if received).
- [ ] Internal approvals: board/management approval of contract (if required by policy or state).
- [ ] Annual report disclosure worksheet tying each disclosure to a source document.
- [ ] Correspondence with vendor regarding compliance remediation.

---

# Output Modes

## Mode 1 — Full Package (default)

All deliverables from Phases 1–4:
1. Relationship classification decision tree (1.1)
2. Fundraiser identity & registration verification matrix (1.2)
3. Filing/disclosure obligation mapping worksheet (1.3)
4. Disclosure data extraction table (2.1)
5. Funds flow & custody-of-funds map (2.2)
6. Mandated-clause compliance checklist (3.1)
7. State-specific clause matrix (3.2)
8. Gap/issue log + remediation plan (4.1)
9. Disclosure-ready narrative snippets (4.2)
10. State reporting data summary sheet (4.3)
11. Evidence binder checklist (4.4)

## Mode 2 — Quick Reference

- Relationship classification decision tree (1.1)
- Top disclosure fields to extract (key fields from 2.1)
- Top 10 Red Flags checklist (below)
- State reporting data summary sheet (4.3)

### Top 10 Red Flags (quick scan)
- [ ] Vendor solicits in a state but is not registered/licensed (or cannot provide ID).
- [ ] Contract does not clearly state compensation or the base for a percentage calculation.
- [ ] Vendor takes custody of funds and contract is silent on segregation/remittance timing.
- [ ] No charity approval right over scripts/solicitation materials.
- [ ] No recordkeeping/accounting obligation and no access/inspection rights.
- [ ] Co-venture ads or promotion terms are unclear (amount/percent/dates not specified).
- [ ] Subcontractors used without charity consent or registration controls.
- [ ] Donor data can be reused/sold; no privacy/security commitments.
- [ ] Termination/cancellation rights missing or inconsistent with state mandates.
- [ ] Operations do not match the contract (actual funds flow differs from contractual terms).

## Mode 3 — Checklist Only

- Baseline mandated-clause compliance checklist (3.1)
- Evidence binder checklist (4.4)

---

# Phase 5: Output Refinement (MANDATORY)

## Checkpoint B — Post-Draft Fit Check

**Does this match your needs?** Choose one or more refinements:

- **A. State-specific deep dive:** Name 1–3 states; I will reshape the clause matrix and disclosures around those states' specific statutory requirements.
- **B. Disclosure-only:** Focus exclusively on annual report fields and draft disclosures; minimize contract-law analysis.
- **C. Contract remediation pack:** Produce amendment-ready clause suggestions and a prioritized redline list to cure identified gaps.
- **D. Multi-state campaign map:** Build a solicitation footprint and triggered filings list (pairs well with @charitable-solicitation-multi-state-scope-map).
- **E. Evidence binder build:** Convert into a document request list and folder structure for audit readiness.
- **F. Deficiency letter:** Generate a memo to the vendor or outside counsel highlighting missing mandatory state clauses and requesting remediation by a specified deadline.

---

# Phase 6: Quality-Control Self-Audit (MANDATORY)

## QC Checklist — Complete Before Finalizing

- [ ] **Assumptions** are explicitly listed at the top and are not contradictory.
- [ ] **Jurisdiction(s)** and **filing targets** are identified or defaulted.
- [ ] **Relationship type** classification is documented with supporting facts from the contract and operations.
- [ ] Each contract has a completed **extraction table row** (or is flagged as missing with a document request issued).
- [ ] **Compensation** terms are extracted and the percentage of gross contributions retained by the charity can be computed (or the gap is flagged).
- [ ] **Custody of funds** is extracted, mapped, and cross-checked against operational evidence.
- [ ] Vendor **registration IDs** are collected, expiration/renewal status noted, and cross-referenced against state registry databases.
- [ ] All items on the **mandated-clause checklist** are marked, and all gaps are logged in the issue log.
- [ ] Gap log includes **owner + deadline + remediation action** for every item.
- [ ] **Disclosure narrative snippets** tie back to identified source documents.
- [ ] **State reporting data summary** maps extracted data to correct form fields.
- [ ] State-specific requirements are **flagged for verification** with statute citations to be added.
- [ ] **Operational alignment** confirmed: actual funds flow and practices match the contractual terms.
- [ ] **Pre-solicitation filing deadlines** (Notices of Intent, contract filings) are checked.
- [ ] Cross-references added where relevant:
  - @charitable-registration-annual-report-prep
  - @vendor-contract-issue-spotting
  - @funds-flow-and-restricted-funds-controls
  - @contract-risk-assessment

---

## Jurisdiction-Specific Note

Charitable solicitation regulation is state-based and can impose **registration, bonding, contract filing, donor disclosure, campaign financial reporting, and pre-solicitation notice** obligations. Requirements differ significantly depending on whether the third party is classified as a professional solicitor, fundraising counsel, or commercial co-venturer. Always verify the applicable definitions and required contract provisions for each state where solicitations occur.

---

## Disclaimer

This skill provides a structured compliance-review workflow and templates for legal and compliance teams. It is not legal advice and does not substitute for state-specific review by qualified counsel specializing in charitable solicitation law. Requirements are subject to frequent legislative change; verify current statutes before relying on any checklist item.
